title: Divergence: D-U-N-S Registration — Standard vs. Expedited
domain: Decision Domain 2: D-U-N-S Number & Business Credit Foundation
reasoning: The Algorithmic Manager recommended expedited D-U-N-S registration on the basis that 3 weeks of accelerated credit-building compounds meaningfully over a 12-month horizon. The Human Executor disagrees: the credit profile will not be used for some time, so the compounding time-value argument does not apply to the current operational reality. $50 toward a 3-week acceleration has near-zero return when the credit facilities it enables are not needed in the near term. The standard free registration achieves the same outcome at $0 cost. This is not a rejection of the credit-building strategy — it is a recalibration of urgency.
maximAlignmentAssessment: The divergence is consistent with the Root Thesis Maxim. Sovereign, self-sustaining systems do not waste resources on acceleration without demonstrated time pressure. Buffett's own principle — cited by the engine — counsels against deploying capital without expected return exceeding cost. At current operational tempo, $50 for 3 weeks produces no measurable return.
